completed.label: Define the next multi-dataset phase after Britain, USA, and France all become live fields
Define a `solid` maturity threshold above `usable draft`
Approve dataset-specific atmospheric theming so each field can feel visually distinct
Define lived voice v2 with stronger persona and multiple social positions per period where the content can support it
Selectively validate Britain, USA, and France toward the `solid` threshold rather than reopening broad enrichment
Implement Force Explorer as the next visual relationship surface and retire the weak detail-panel force card
Design an `In motion` or `Interactive time` concept with Race as the first mode, trails enabled, and architecture that can later compare same-time-range datasets
Define the v1 Race mode as an expansive modal with autoplay, interpolated motion, pinned-force emphasis, and trails
Implement a derived-insight layer for clusters, co-movement, candidate pressure relationships, and a first public USA pilot with a separate Insights Lab surface
Define the whole-tool public-language and visual-grammar rule so public-facing insight is concrete, visual, and understandable to non-specialists
Extend the schema and live dataset plans to support population as a first-class contextual series, beginning in detail and compare
Review the derived insight output editorially, especially internal cross-dataset cousin candidates, before any wider public release
Decide whether the USA insight pilot now promotes into the full three-dataset live system for the approved public surfaces
Codify dataset acquisition, assembly, validation, and repeatable promotion so future dataset work does not depend on project memory
Review the internal Britain, France, and cross-dataset packs to decide which structural cousins and echo-support signals are honest enough for later public release
Review whether any further cross-dataset cousins or echo-support signals are strong, legible, and public-safe enough to follow the first USA-France reveal
Reopen cousin and echo-support release only if a second reveal adds genuinely new public value rather than repeating the first pattern

next.label: Review the first live Race modal in use and decide what it teaches before locking the Tension mode or same-time-range dataset comparison layout
next.reason: Race now exists as the first motion surface, so the next useful step is to learn from it before deciding the exact Tension chart or future comparison view.

next.label: Review and sharpen the generated USA insight prompts, family labels, and surfaced relationship wording so the first public pilot feels genuinely editorial rather than merely computational
next.reason: The first derived-insight layer is live, but its public value now depends on editorial quality, not just on the presence of statistics.

next.label: Apply the first public-language and visual-grammar pass across Insights Lab, the detail-panel insight hook, Compare, and the most visible entry labels
next.reason: The product now needs public-facing clarity and visual gestalt more urgently than more analytical depth.

next.label: Design the first compact population chart for Compare so scale change becomes visible and startling without cluttering the main Loom
next.reason: Population is now in the data model and compare is the best first chart surface for making scale growth feel revealing rather than decorative.

next.label: Run selective validation only where a concrete blocker is keeping Britain, USA, or France below the `solid` threshold
next.reason: Validation should now be targeted and evidence-led rather than broad and open-ended.
